title: Wholesome Smoky Canadian Tofu Bowl
description: A tangy, shaggy bowl featuring smoky barbecue tofu over quinoa, roasted squash, and steamed broccoli, perfect for a date-night Canadian vibe.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400°F and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Toss the butternut squash cubes in a tablespoon of sesame oil and roast for 20 minutes until tender and slightly caramelized.
Heat a non‑stick skillet over medium heat and add the tofu cubes, cooking until golden brown on all sides.
In a small bowl, whisk together the barbecue sauce, maple syrup, grated ginger, soy sauce, and remaining tablespoon of sesame oil.
Pour the sauce over the browned tofu and stir to coat, cooking for an additional 2 minutes until the sauce thickens.
Meanwhile, steam the broccoli florets until bright green and just tender, about 5 minutes.
Arrange the cooked quinoa at the bottom of a bowl, then top with roasted squash, steamed broccoli, and saucy tofu.
Sprinkle toasted sesame seeds over the entire bowl for an extra smoky crunch.
Serve the bowl warm and enjoy the smoky, tangy flavors that hint at a Canadian winter night.
Optional: garnish with chopped scallions for a fresh finish.
